title: NOC 13102 – Payroll Administrators | GTR Immigration
description: Payroll Administrators are essential to Canadian organizations, ensuring accurate salary processing, statutory deductions, and employee benefit administration. Classified under NOC 13102, this occupation remains in steady demand nationwide and offers strong immigration prospects through Canada’s skilled worker pathways and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training

common_job_titles: Benefits officer – payroll administration Pay advisor Pay and benefits administrator Pay and benefits clerk Pay clerk Payroll clerk Payroll officer Salary administration officer
duties: Maintain employee attendance, leave, and overtime records
Prepare earnings statements showing gross pay, deductions, and net salary
Process payroll, bonuses, and vacation payments
Administer benefit documentation for pensions, insurance, and leave programs
Prepare year-end filings, Records of Employment, and tax statements
Respond to payroll and benefits inquiries
Compile payroll statistics and reconciliation reports
Develop or update payroll policies and procedures
employment_requirements: College courses in payroll, bookkeeping, or accounting, or relevant experience
Experience with payroll systems and software
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 23,100 new positions for Payroll Administrators, compared with 22,600 job seekers—indicating steady prospects for overseas professionals who plan province selection carefully.
additional_information: Payroll Administrators are employed across payroll service firms, corporations, government bodies, and public institutions.

Consistent demand across industriesGrowing compliance and payroll-technology requirementsEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit pathwaysAnnual earnings typically ranging from $36,600 to $72,600 CAD
